definition: To give up or relinquish control of, to surrender or to give oneself over, or to yield to one's emotions.

definition: Countries or lands abroad.

definition: That which is independent of context-dependent interpretation, inviolate, fundamental.

definition: Worthy, decent, sure of being accepted or received with at least moderate pleasure

definition: To go with or attend as a companion or associate; to keep company with; to go along with.

definition: Telling the truth or giving a true result; exact; not defective or faulty

definition: Accusation.

definition: To admit the knowledge of; to recognize as a fact or truth; to declare one's belief in
example: to acknowledge the being of a god

definition: To get.

definition: An actual, real one; notably:

definition: To make suitable; to make to correspond; to fit or suit

definition: Something added.

definition: A forward move; improvement or progression.

definition: (often in the plural) Something which is done or is to be done; business of any kind, commercial, professional, or public.

definition: (temporal location) At a later or succeeding time.

definition: A temporally organized plan for matters to be attended to.

definition: Help; assistance; succor, relief.

To change the form or structure of.
A strong feeling of displeasure, hostility or antagonism towards someone or something, usually combined with an urge to harm.

definition: A figure formed by two rays which start from a common point (a plane angle) or by three planes that intersect (a solid angle).

definition: A day that is an exact number of years (to the day) since a given significant event occurred. Often preceded by an ordinal number indicating the number of years.

definition: An annual publication; a book, periodical, journal, report, comic book, yearbook, etc., which is published serially once a year, which may or may not be in addition to regular weekly or monthly publication.

definition: Nervous and worried.

definition: Capable of being seen, or easily seen; open to view; visible to the eye, eyely; within sight or view.
Plainly; clearly; manifestly; evidently.

definition: To make suitable; to suit.

definition: To take by the arm; to take up in one's arms.

definition: Man-made; of artifice.

definition: Having or revealing creative skill.

definition: The act of assessing or an amount (of tax, levy or duty etc) assessed.

definition: To join in or form a league, union, or association.

definition: The act of associating.

A structure that bars passage.
In a fundamental, essential or basic manner

definition: The act of begging; an imploring request.

definition: A living creature.

definition: The unknown.

definition: A covering for a window to keep out light. The covering may be made of cloth or of narrow slats that can block light or allow it to pass.
example: a duck blind

definition: Evidence of a long-term debt, by which the bond issuer (the borrower) is obliged to pay interest when due, and repay the principal at maturity, as specified on the face of the bond certificate. The rights of the holder are specified in the bond indenture, which contains the legal terms and conditions under which the bond was issued. Bonds are available in two forms: registered bonds, and bearer bonds.
example: Investors face a quandary. Cash offers a return of virtually zero in many developed countries; government-bond yields may have risen in recent weeks but they are still unattractive. Equities have suffered two big bear markets since 2000 and are wobbling again. It is hardly surprising that pension funds, insurers and endowments are searching for new sources of return.

definition: Either of the two organs on the front of a female human's chest, which contain the mammary glands; also the analogous organs in males.
example: Tanya's breasts grew remarkably during pregnancy.

definition: A writ summoning one to answer to any action.

definition: A shallow lake, one of a number of bodies of water in eastern Norfolk and Suffolk.
example: the broad expanse of ocean

definition: A transmission of a radio or television programme intended to be received by anyone with a receiver.

definition: The amount of money or resources earmarked for a particular institution, activity or time-frame.

definition: A projectile, usually of metal, shot from a gun at high speed.

definition: A group of similar things, either growing together, or in a cluster or clump, usually fastened together.

definition: To determine the value of something or the solution to something by a mathematical process.

definition: A cancellation (US); (nonstandard in some kinds of English).

definition: A disease in which the cells of a tissue undergo uncontrolled (and often rapid) proliferation.

definition: A person presiding over a meeting.
A distinguishing feature of a person or thing.

definition: A leader or head of a group of people, organisation, etc.

definition: That which attends, or relates to, or in some way affects, a fact or event; an attendant thing or state of things.

definition: Having to do with people and government office as opposed to the military or religion.

definition: The act of combining, the state of being combined or the result of combining.

definition: A sending or mission (to do or accomplish something).

definition: The act or an instance of committing, putting in charge, keeping, or trust, especially:

definition: To make complex; to modify so as to make something intricate or difficult.

definition: A smaller, self-contained part of a larger entity. Often refers to a manufactured object that is part of a larger device.

definition: The act, process or ability of concentrating; the process of becoming concentrated, or the state of being concentrated.
An abstract and general idea; an abstraction.
That which affects one’s welfare or happiness. A matter of interest to someone. The adposition before the matter of interest is usually over, about or for.
example: Judy's eyes filled with concern as she listened to the news report.

definition: To relate or belong to; to have reference to or connection with; to affect the interest of; to be of importance to.

definition: The act or method of controlling or directing

definition: Self-assurance.

definition: A coming together of two or more people; a meeting.

definition: The part of the mind that is aware of itself; the consciousness.

definition: A person who favors maintenance of the status quo.

definition: The thought process of considering, of taking multiple or specified factors into account (with of being the main corresponding adposition).

definition: Something constructed from parts.

definition: (except in phrases) Satisfaction, contentment; pleasure.

definition: To encounter or discover by accident; to happen upon.

example: He hesitated whether to accept the offer or not; men often hesitate in forming a judgment.

definition: A small valley between mountains.
example: He built himself a cabin in a hollow high up in the Rockies.

definition: A thing that is extremely holy; used almost exclusively in Holy of Holies.
example: I'm planning to visit the holy city of Jerusalem this Christmas.

definition: Recognition of importance or value; respect; veneration (of someone, usually for being morally upright or successful)
example: The crowds gave the returning general much honor and praise.

definition: Collectively, all the persons who live in a given house; a family including attendants, servants etc.; a domestic or family establishment.
example: a household word; a household name

definition: To keep within a structure or container.
example: The car is housed in the garage.

definition: The quality of being amusing, comical, funny.
example: She has a great sense of humour, and I always laugh a lot whenever we get together.

definition: Full of humor or arousing laughter; funny.
The act of finding and killing a wild animal, either for sport or with the intention of using its parts to make food, clothes, etc.
example: Her uncle will go out and hunt for deer, now that it is open season.

definition: To shed light upon.
example: We illustrate our definitions by including quotations or simple examples.

definition: The act of illustrating; the act of making clear and distinct; education; also, the state of being illustrated, or of being made clear and distinct.
The image-making power of the mind; the act of mentally creating or reproducing an object not previously perceived; the ability to create such images.
example: Imagination is one of the most advanced human faculties.

definition: Restless and intolerant of delays.
example: impatient speeches or replies

definition: (of a proposition) to have as a necessary consequence
example: The proposition that "all dogs are mammals" implies that my dog is a mammal

definition: To establish or apply by authority.
example: Congress imposed new tariffs.

definition: The act of impressing.
example: You impressed me with your command of Urdu.

definition: To affect (someone) strongly and often favourably.
example: You impressed me with your command of Urdu.

definition: A unit of length equal to one twelfth of a foot, or exactly 2.54 centimetres.
example: "Don't move an inch!"

definition: An event or occurrence.
example: It was an incident that he hoped to forget.

definition: Money one earns by working or by capitalising on the work of others.
Increasing in amount or intensity
example: His rage only increased when I told him of the lost money.

definition: (19th-mid 20th century) An employee in industry.
example: I wish they'd play more industrial in this club.

definition: The act or process of infecting.
To instruct, train (usually in matters of knowledge).
example: His sense of religion informs everything he writes.

definition: The first letter of a word or a name.
example: You can get your initials printed at the top.

definition: At the beginning.
example: Initially, I found city life incredibly difficult, but now I'm used to it.

definition: A beginning; a first move.
An inner part.
example: inner door;  inner room;  inner sanctum;  inner surface

definition: The act of inquiring; a seeking of information by asking questions; interrogation; a question or questioning.
example: Scientific inquiry

definition: A sight or view of the interior of anything; a deep inspection or view; introspection; frequently used with into.
(with on or upon or (that + ordinary verb form)) To hold up a claim emphatically.
example: I insist that my secretary dresses nicely.

definition: To infuse into the mind; to communicate to the spirit; to convey, as by a divine or supernatural influence; to disclose preternaturally; to produce in, as by inspiration.
example: Elders should inspire children with sentiments of virtue.

definition: Installer. A software utility that installs an application.
example: After inserting the disk, you need to run the install.

definition: Urgency of manner or words; an urgent request; insistence.
example: It sends some precious instance of itself/ After the thing it loves. Hamlet IV. v. ca. 1602

definition: An organization founded to promote a cause
example: I work in a medical research institute.

definition: A custom or practice of a society or community.
example: He's been in an institution since the crash.

definition: A means of indemnity against a future occurrence of an uncertain event.
example: The car was totalled, but fortunately I had insurance.

definition: (usually followed by the particle "to") To hope; to wish (something, or something to be accomplished); be intent upon
example: He intends to go to university.

definition: Strained; tightly drawn.
example: intense study;  intense thought

definition: Inside of something
example: We saw the internal compartments.

definition: To explain or tell the meaning of; to translate orally into intelligible or familiar language or terms. applied especially to language, but also to dreams, signs, conduct, mysteries, etc.
example: to interpret an Indian speech

definition: An event that causes a computer or other device to temporarily cease what it was doing and attend to a condition.
example: The interrupt caused the packet handler routine to run.

definition: The act of investigating; the process of inquiring into or following up; research, especially patient or thorough inquiry or examination
example: Despite thorough investigation, the perpetrator of the attacks remains unknown.

definition: The act of investing, or state of being invested.
example: Giving your children a good education is a wise long-term investment.

definition: A feeling of extreme happiness or cheerfulness, especially related to the acquisition or expectation of something good.
example: They will be a source of strength and joy in your life.

definition: The act of judging.
example: a man of judgment

definition: A younger person.
example: four years his junior

definition: The state or characteristic of being just or fair.
example: the justice of a description

definition: To provide an acceptable explanation for.
example: How can you justify spending so much money on clothes?

definition: Effort expended on a particular task; toil, work.
example: I think we've all got the idea. There's no need to labour the point.

definition: A portion of land or territory which the eye can comprehend in a single view, including all the objects it contains.
example: The software patent landscape has changed considerably in the last years

definition: In a widespread or large manner.
example: They were largely successful in their efforts.

definition: The movement of a vessel from land into the water; especially, the sliding on ways from the stocks on which it is built. (Compare: to splash a ship.)
example: book launch

definition: The capacity of someone to lead others.
A group or association of cooperating members.
example: the League of Nations

definition: (of an object taller than its width and depth) An inclination away from the vertical.
example: The trees had various leans toward gaps in the canopy.

definition: A legal document giving official permission to do something; a permit.
example: In order to enter the building, I need to show my license.

definition: To restrict; not to allow to go beyond a certain bound, to set boundaries.
example: I'm limiting myself to two drinks tonight.

definition: Term of address.
example: He's not expected to live for more than a few months.

definition: A burden; a weight to be carried.
example: I struggled up the hill with the heavy load in my rucksack.

definition: An act or instance of lending, an act or instance of granting something for temporary use.
example: Because of the loan that John made to me, I was able to pay my tuition for the upcoming semester.

definition: In agreement with the principles of logic.
example: Logical memory appears contiguous to an application program, but may well be stored on several physical devices, including in RAM and on hard-disks, as determined by the operating system.

definition: Becoming evident after a relatively long time period.
The release of an arrow.
example: This wheelbarrow has a loose wheel.

definition: The master of the servants of a household; the master of a feudal manor
Situated close to, or even below, the ground or another normal reference plane; not high or lofty.
example: a low wall  a low shelf

definition: A biological organ of vertebrates that controls breathing and oxygenates the blood.
example: He hasn't the lungs to play long rallies like he use to.

definition: To support (someone), to back up or assist (someone) in an action.
More than half (50%) of some group.
example: The majority agreed that the new proposal was the best.

definition: (physical) Matter, material.
example: I mass 70 kilograms.

definition: A homogeneous mass of rock, not layered and without an obvious crystal structure.
example: karst massives in western Georgia

definition: Someone who has control over something or someone.
example: Mark Twain was a master of fiction.

definition: To agree; to be equal; to correspond.
example: Their interests didn't match, so it took a long time to agree what to do together.

definition: The highest limit
example: Use the proper dose for the maximum effect.

definition: The chief executive of the municipal government of a city, borough, &c., formerly usually appointed as a caretaker by European royal courts but now usually appointed or elected locally.
To lament.
example: Does she really mean what she said to him last night?

definition: The act of measuring.
Molten material, the product of melting.
example: I melted butter to make a cake.

definition: Armed forces.
example: He spent six years in the military.

definition: Any naturally occurring inorganic material that has a (more or less) definite chemical composition and characteristic physical properties.
The lowest limit.
example: We need a minimum of three staff members on duty at all time.

definition: A person who is trained to preach, to perform religious ceremonies, and to afford pastoral care at a Protestant church.
example: The minister said a prayer on behalf of the entire congregation.

definition: A person who is below the age of majority, consent, criminal responsibility or other adult responsibilities and accountabilities.
example: It is illegal to sell weapons to minors under the age of eighteen.

definition: The state of being a minor; youth, the period of a person's life prior to reaching adulthood.
example: Most people agreed, but a sizable minority were upset by the decision.

definition: A set of tasks that fulfills a purpose or duty; an assignment set by an employer, or by oneself.
example: Many cities across the Americas grew from Spanish missions.

definition: To stir together.
example: Mix the eggs and milk with the flour until the consistency is smooth.

definition: To change part of.
Someone who watches over something; a person in charge of something or someone.
example: The camp monitors look after the children during the night, when the teachers are asleep.

definition: (of a narrative) The ethical significance or practical lesson.
example: a candidate with strong morals

definition: A machine or device that converts other energy forms into mechanical energy, or imparts motion.
example: Nice motor!

definition: A hill or mountain.
example: the mount of Jupiter

definition: A whole number that can be divided by another number with no remainder.
example: 14, 21 and 70 are multiples of 7

definition: An act or instance of multiplying.
example: when you multiply 3 by 7, you get 21;  he multiplied several numbers

definition: Of unknown origin.
example: He received a mysterious phone call this morning.

definition: A bundle of neurons with their connective tissue sheaths, blood vessels and lymphatics.
example: The nerves can be seen through the skin.

definition: In spite of what preceded; yet.
A demon or monster, thought to plague people while they slept and cause a feeling of suffocation and terror during sleep.
example: I had a nightmare that I tried to run but could neither move nor breathe.

definition: Mental apprehension of whatever may be known, thought, or imagined; idea, concept.
example: Yankee notions

definition: Indefinitely large numerically, many.
example: There are numerous definitions of the word 'man'.

definition: To do as ordered by (a person, institution etc), to act according to the bidding of.
A material object that physically exists.
The act of binding oneself by a social, legal, or moral tie to someone.
example: X shall be entitled to subcontract its obligation to provide the Support Services. <>

definition: The act of observing, and the fact of being observed (see observance)
To notice or view, especially carefully or with attention to detail.
example: From this vantage point we can observe the behavior of the animals in their natural habitat.

definition: To get hold of; to gain possession of, to procure; to acquire, in any way.
On the occasion of something else happening; incidentally, by the way.
example: He took an occasional glass of wine.

definition: To hurt the feelings of; to displease; to make angry; to insult.
example: Your accusations offend me deeply.

definition: The act of offending:
An attack.
example: The Marines today launched a major offensive.

definition: To make something accessible or allow for passage by moving from a shut position.
example: Turn the doorknob to open the door.

definition: To perform a work or labour; to exert power or strength, physical or mechanical; to act.
example: to operate a machine

definition: One who opposes another; one who works or takes a position against someone or something; one who attempts to stop the progress of someone or something.
example: During the crackdown, many opponents of the regime were arrested.

definition: To attempt to stop the progression of; to resist or antagonize by physical means, or by arguments, etc.; to contend against.
example: There is still time to oppose this plan.

definition: To attempt to stop the progression of; to resist or antagonize by physical means, or by arguments, etc.; to contend against.
example: There is still time to oppose this plan.

definition: The action of opposing or of being in conflict.
A larger part of an organism, composed of tissues that perform similar functions.
example: bodily organs

definition: The beginning of something.
Other than supposed; different.
example: He said he didn’t do it, but the evidence was otherwise.

definition: That which is produced or occurs as a result of an event or process.
example: A quality automobile is the outcome of the work of skilled engineers and thousands of workers.

definition: An outer part.
example: They wrote the law to give those organizations an out.

definition: A line marking the boundary of an object figure.
example: the outline of a speech

definition: A garment worn over other clothing to protect it; a coverall or boiler suit. A garment, for manual labor or for casual wear, often made of a single piece of fabric, with long legs and a bib upper, supported from the shoulders with straps, and having several large pockets and loops for carrying tools.
example: Overall, there is not enough evidence to form a clear conclusion.

definition: To be under an obligation to give something back to someone or to perform some action for someone.
A (usually) rectangular section of a surface, or of a covering or of a wall, fence etc.
example: Behind the picture was a panel on the wall.

definition: One who participates.
example: All participants must adhere to the rules of the competition.

definition: (focus) Especially, extremely.
example: The apéritifs were particularly stimulating.

definition: In part, or to some degree, but not completely.
example: they partly finished the house so they could move in before winter;  the car was partly visible in the garage

definition: A paragraph or section of text or music with particular meaning.
example: She struggled to play the difficult passages.

definition: A chemical hair treatment imparting or removing curliness, whose effects typically last for a period of weeks; a perm.
example: Nothing in this world is truly permanent.

definition: An artifact or document rendering something allowed or legal.
example: A construction permit can be obtained from the town offices.

definition: A view, vista or outlook.
example: a perspective drawing

definition: A thing or being, event or process, perceptible through senses; or a fact or occurrence thereof.
example: An electromagnetic phenomenon.

definition: The love of wisdom.
example: Philosophy is often divided into five major branches: logic, metaphysics, epistemology, ethics and aesthetics.

definition: A mass of things heaped together; a heap.
example: When we were looking for a new housemate, we put the nice woman on the "maybe" pile, and the annoying guy on the "no" pile

definition: A sticky, gummy substance secreted by trees; sap.
example: It is hard to get this pitch off my hand.

definition: Flat, level.
example: Throughout high school she worried that she had a rather plain face.

definition: To extend the index finger in the direction of something in order to show where it is or to draw attention to it.
example: It's rude to point at other people.

definition: The quality or state of being popular; especially, the state of being esteemed by, or of being in favor with, the people at large
example: Politicians are rarely known for their popularity.

definition: Common cold, head cold; catarrh.
To have; to have ownership of.
example: He does not even possess a working telephone.

definition: Currently unrealized ability (with the most common adposition being to)
example: Even from a young age it was clear that she had the potential to become a great musician.

definition: Commendation; favourable representation in words
example: Be sure to praise Bobby for his excellent work at school this week.

definition: A pregnant person.
example: I went to the doctor and, guess what, I'm pregnant!

definition: The act of preparing or getting ready.
A sweet spread made of any of a variety of fruits.
example: Every people have the right to preserve its identity and culture.

definition: The first hour of daylight; the first canonical hour.
example: 3 is a prime.

definition: A fundamental assumption or guiding belief.
example: We need some sort of principles to reason from.

definition: An item's relative importance.
example: He set his e-mail message's priority to high.

definition: The state of being secluded from the presence, sight, or knowledge of others.
example: I need my privacy, so please stay out of my room.

definition: A particular method for performing a task.
example: Ensure that you follow procedure when accessing customers' personal information.

definition: An effort, process, or operation designed to establish or discover a fact or truth; an act of testing; a test; a trial.
example: a proof load; a proof charge

definition: Something which is proposed, or offered for consideration or acceptance
An objective or aim.
example: I propose going to see a film.

definition: The region which the eye overlooks at one time; view; scene; outlook.
The process of keeping (something or someone) safe.
example: Raincoats give protection from rain.

definition: An expert in the field of psychology.
The study of the human mind.
The act of publishing printed or other matter.
The acquisition of title to, or property in, anything for a price; buying for money or its equivalent.
example: They offer a free hamburger with the purchase of a drink.

definition: One who, or that which, is pure.
example: Tiger Woods pured his first drive straight down the middle of the fairway.

definition: To follow urgently, originally with intent to capture or harm; to chase.
example: Her rival pursued a quite different course.

definition: Strong of its kind or in character; unmitigated; virulent; thorough; utter (used of negative things).
example: rank nonsense

definition: (often in the plural) a rough section of a river or stream which is difficult to navigate due to the swift and turbulent motion of the water.
example: a rapid stream

definition: With speed; in a rapid manner.
example: She packed her case rapidly and hurried out.

definition: (sugar refining, sugar trade) An unprocessed sugar; a batch of such.
example: raw cane sugar

definition: Expressed or represented as being accurate, practicable, or not idealistic.
example: A realistic appraisal of the situation.

definition: Having the faculty of reason; rational, reasoning.
example: a reasonable demand, amount, or price

definition: The action or fact of calling someone or something back.
example: He was recalled to service after his retirement.

definition: Recovery.
example: After days of inquiries, he finally recovered his lost wallet.

definition: The act, process, or result of reducing.
example: A 5% reduction in robberies

definition: A steady look, a gaze.
example: He is held in great regard in Whitehall.

definition: An entity or event with scope limited to a single region.
A machine that tabulates the amount of sales transactions, makes a permanent and cumulative record of them, and has a drawer in which cash can be kept.
example: The teacher took the register by calling out each child's name.

definition: Emotional pain on account of something done or experienced in the past, with a wish that it had been different; a looking back with dissatisfaction or with longing.
example: He regretted his words.

definition: The act of regulating or the condition of being regulated.
Proportionally, in relation to some larger scale thing.
example: He measured his success relatively, that is, competitively.

definition: Directly related, connected, or pertinent to a topic.
example: His mother provided some relevant background information concerning his medical condition.

definition: The removal of stress or discomfort.
example: I sighed with relief when I found out that my daughter hadn't got lost, but was waiting for me at home.

definition: (with on or upon, formerly also with in) to trust; to have confidence in; to depend.
An act of pointing out or noticing; notice or observation.
example: a biting remark

definition: A delegate.
example: Are you sure this paper is representative of your child's writing?

definition: What somebody is known for.

A necessity or prerequisite; something required or obligatory. Its adpositions are generally of in relation to who or what has given it, on in relation to whom or what it is given to, and for in relation to what is required.
example: There was a requirement of the government on citizens for paying taxes.

definition: An act or episode of rescuing, saving.
example: The rescue of Jerusalem was the original motive of the Crusaders

definition: (behaviour) Restriction.
example: We reserve the right to make modifications.

definition: A person, animal or plant living at a certain location or in a certain area.
example: The tiger lily is a resident of Asia.

definition: A protective coating or covering.
Determination; will power.
example: It took all my resolve to go through with the surgery.

definition: A place where people go for recreation, especially one with facilities such as lodgings, entertainment, and a relaxing environment.
example: to have resort to violence

definition: To keep in possession or use.
The outer side of a window or door frame; the jamb.
example: The comedian had been telling us about his sleep being disturbed by noise. Then came the reveal: he was sleeping on a bed in a department store.

definition: A political upheaval in a government or nation state characterized by great change.
Something of value given in return for an act.
example: For catching the thief, you'll get a nice reward.

definition: The variation of strong and weak elements (such as duration, accent) of sounds, notably in speech or music, over time; a beat or meter.
example: Dance to the rhythm of the music.

definition: To free (something) from a hindrance or annoyance.
example: We're trying to rid the world of poverty.

definition: The part of a plant, generally underground, that anchors and supports the plant body, absorbs and stores water and nutrients, and in some plants is able to perform vegetative reproduction.
example: This tree's roots can go as deep as twenty metres underground.

definition: An act of rubbing.
example: Give that lamp a good rub and see if any genies come out

definition: Pliable material derived from the sap of the rubber tree; a hydrocarbon polymer of isoprene.
example: Jones toes the rubber and then fires to the plate.

definition: A person from the countryside; a rustic.
Any of several stiff plants of the genus Juncus, or the family Juncaceae, having hollow or pithy stems and small flowers, and often growing in marshes or near water.
A moon or other smaller body orbiting a larger one.
example: A spent upper stage is a derelict satellite.

definition: To do enough for; to meet the needs of; to fulfill the wishes or requirements of.
example: I'm not satisfied with the quality of the food here.

definition: To do enough for; to meet the needs of; to fulfill the wishes or requirements of.
example: I'm not satisfied with the quality of the food here.

definition: To prevent harm or difficulty.
example: The shift of the supplier gave us a saving of 10 percent.

definition: A ladder; a series of steps; a means of ascending.
example: Please rate your experience on a scale from 1 to 10.

definition: Section
example: public sector;  private sector

definition: To make safe; to relieve from apprehensions of, or exposure to, danger; to guard; to protect.
example: to secure a creditor against loss; to secure a debt by a mortgage

definition: The operation of navigating through a stream.
example: I seek wisdom.

definition: To choose one or more elements of a set, especially a set of options.
example: He looked over the menu, and selected the roast beef.

definition: A process by which heritable traits conferring survival and reproductive advantage to individuals, or related individuals, tend to be passed on to succeeding generations and become more frequent in a population, whereas other less favourable traits tend to become eliminated; the differential survival and reproduction of phenotypes.
example: The large number of good candidates made selection difficult.

definition: One individual's personality, character, demeanor, or disposition.
example: one's true self; one's better self; one's former self

definition: In some bicameral legislative systems, the upper house or chamber.
A member, normally elected, in the house or chamber of a legislature called a senate. The legislatures of the United States and Canada have senators.
An old person.
example: He was four years her senior.

definition: A person with a paranormal sensitivity to something that most cannot perceive.
example: Max is very sensitive; he cried today because of the bad news.

definition: A set of things next to each other in a set order; a series
example: Complete the listed tasks in sequence.

definition: An informal gathering of musicians to play music, especially improvised jazz or a similar genre.
example: "Are we having a recording session?" / "Yes. We've even got some session musicians to provide some brass."

definition: To conclude or resolve (something):
example: to settle her estate

definition: Very bad or intense.
example: a severe taskmaster

definition: Darkness where light, particularly sunlight, is blocked.
example: The old oak tree gave shade in the heat of the day.

definition: A dark image projected onto a surface where light (or other radiation) is blocked by the shade of an object.
example: My shadow lengthened as the sun began to set.

definition: (modal, auxiliary verb, defective) Used before a verb to indicate the simple future tense in the first person singular or plural.
example: I hope that we shall win the game.

definition: A shallow portion of an otherwise deep body of water.
example: The ship ran aground in an unexpected shallow.

definition: Uncomfortable or painful feeling due to recognition or consciousness of one's own impropriety or dishonor or something being exposed that should have been kept private.
example: The teenager couldn’t bear the shame of introducing his parents.

definition: A refuge, haven or other cover or protection from something.
example: During the rainstorm, we sheltered under a tree.

definition: A sudden, heavy impact.
example: The train hit the buffers with a great shock.

definition: To cause to be emotionally shocked, to cause (someone) to feel surprised and upset.
example: The disaster shocked the world.

definition: To launch a projectile.
example: The land shoots into a promontory.

definition: To launch a projectile.
example: The land shoots into a promontory.

definition: That which has significance; a sign; a token; a symbol.
example: a significant word or sound; a significant look

definition: In a significant manner or to a significant extent.
example: a significant word or sound; a significant look

definition: The absence of any sound.
example: When the motor stopped, the silence was almost deafening.

definition: A fine fiber excreted by the silkworm or other arthropod (such as a spider).
example: The thread made of silk was barely visible.

definition: Genuine; meaning what one says or does; heartfelt.
example: I believe he is sincere in his offer to help.

definition: An item of play equipment that children can climb up and then slide down again.
example: The long, red slide was great fun for the kids.

definition: The act of slighting; a deliberate act of neglect or discourtesy.
example: a slight but graceful woman

definition: A thin, slippery mix of clay and water.
Of or pertaining to the sun; proceeding from the sun
example: solar influence

definition: More or less; a certain quantity or degree; a part, more or less; something.
example: The crowd was somewhat larger than expected, perhaps due to the good weather.

definition: The spirit or essence of a person usually thought to consist of one's thoughts and personality. Often believed to live on after the person's death.
example: Fifty souls were lost when the ship sank.

definition: Someone who is an expert in, or devoted to, some specific branch of study or research.
example: The tree Lepidothamnus laxifolius is a high alpine specialist found in high-altitude bog communities and in scrub.

definition: Type or kind. (Compare race.)
example: the male species

definition: A Christian religious song, especially one in an African-American style, or a similar non-religious song.
example: He assigns supremacy to the pope in spirituals, and to the emperor in temporals. — Lowell.

definition: A crack or longitudinal fissure.
example: He’s got a nasty split.

definition: A person or organisation with some sort of responsibility for another person or organisation, especially where the responsibility has a religious, legal, or financial aspect.
example: He was my sponsor when I applied to join the club.

definition: A building, wing or dependency set apart and adapted for lodging and feeding (and training) animals with hoofs, especially horses.
example: There were stalls for fourteen horses in the squire's stables.

definition: A persistent gaze.
example: the stares of astonished passers-by

definition: A person’s condition, position or standing relative to that of others.
example: Superstition is highly correlated with economic status.

definition: A rest or support, as for the hand, a tool, or a piece of work.
example: Hold the ladder steady while I go up.

definition: An artificial metal produced from iron, harder and more elastic than elemental iron; used figuratively as a symbol of hardness.
The steep side of a mountain etc.; a slope or acclivity.
example: a steep hill or mountain; a steep roof; a steep ascent; a steep barometric gradient

definition: A sticky note, such as a post-it note.
example: Her desk is covered with yellow stickies.

definition: An average person, usually male, of no particular distinction, skill, or education, often a working stiff or lucky stiff.
example: A Working Stiff's Manifesto: A Memoir of Thirty Jobs I Quit, Nine That Fired Me, and Three I Can't Remember was published in 2003.

definition: A store or supply.
example: The store stocks all kinds of dried vegetables.

definition: A small river; a large creek; a body of moving water confined by banks.
example: He poured the milk in a thin stream from the jug to the glass.

definition: An act of stretching.
example: I was right in the middle of a stretch when the phone rang.

definition: Strained; drawn close; tight.
example: strict embrace

definition: A status resulting from a batter swinging and missing a pitch, or not swinging at a pitch when the ball goes in the strike zone, or hitting a foul ball that is not caught.
example: Thus hand strikes now include single knuckle strikes, knife hand strikes, finger strikes, ridge hand strikes etc., and leg strikes include front kicks, knee strikes, axe kicks, ...

definition: A contortion of the body in an attempt to escape or to perform a difficult task.
example: During the centuries, the people of Ireland struggled constantly to assert their right to govern themselves.

definition: To yield or give way to another.
example: They will not submit to the destruction of their rights.

definition: A quantity obtained by addition or aggregation.
example: The sum of 3 and 4 is 7.

definition: A procedure involving major incisions to remove, repair, or replace a part of a body.
example: Many times surgery is necessary to prevent cancer from spreading.

definition: Anything, such as a fence or border, that surrounds something.
example: to surround the world

definition: To encircle something or simultaneously extend in all directions.
example: to surround the world

definition: A person who is suspected of something, in particular of committing a crime.
example: to suspect the presence of disease

definition: To take an oath, to promise.
A single action of sweeping.
example: Give the front steps a quick sweep to get rid of those fallen leaves.

definition: A feeling of pity or sorrow for the suffering or distress of another; compassion.
example: Many people in Hollywood were blacklisted merely because they were suspected of Communist sympathies.

definition: An account of an asserted fact or circumstance; a rumour; a report, especially an idle or malicious story; a piece of gossip or slander; a lie.
example: Don't tell tales!

definition: A closed container for liquids or gases.
example: I burned three tanks of gas on the drive to New York.

definition: A hole or break caused by tearing.
example: A small tear is easy to mend, if it is on the seam.

definition: One serving for a limited time; short-term employee.
Attempted remediation of a health problem following a diagnosis, usually synonymous with treatment.
An expression of intent to injure or punish another.
To make a threat against someone; to use threats.
example: He threatened me with a knife.

definition: (manner) In this way or manner.
example: If you throw the ball thus, as I’m showing you, you’ll have better luck hitting the target.

definition: A specific pitch.
example: Her rousing speech gave an upbeat tone to the rest of the evening.

definition: A person who obtains things by force; a thug or bully.
example: They were doing fine until they encountered a bunch of toughs from the opposition.

definition: The act of conveying or removing something from one place, person or thing to another.
example: to transfer the laws of one country to another; to transfer suspicion

definition: An operation (often an integration) that converts one function into another.
example: The alchemists sought to transform lead into gold.

definition: The process of change from one form, state, style or place to another.
example: The soldier was transitioned from a combat role to a strategic role.

definition: An opportunity to test something out; a test.
example: They will perform the trials for the new equipment next week.

definition: A tropical plant.
example: tropical fruit

definition: (manner) In accordance with the facts; truthfully, accurately.
example: That is truly all I know.

definition: Confidence in or reliance on some person or quality.
example: He needs to regain her trust if he is ever going to win her back.

definition: A melody.
example: Your engine needs a good tune.

definition: An underground or underwater passage.
Indicating the last item.
example: Firstly,… Secondly,… Ultimately,…

definition: Unconscious mind
example: After the anesthetist administered the general anesthetic the patient was unconscious.

definition: Not expected, anticipated or foreseen.
A thing without a like; something unequalled or unparallelled.
example: Every person has a unique life, therefore every person has a unique journey. ― Gary Cook

definition: Our universe, the sum of everything that exists in the cosmos, including time and space itself.
example: Powerful telescopes look far back into the distant reaches of the Universe.

definition: To undo the process of knowing, to lose knowledge of something.
A stimulant, such as amphetamine, that increases energy and decreases appetite.
The upper part; the top.
example: with upward eye; with upward course

definition: Related to the (or any) city.
A strong desire; an itch to do something.
example: to urge an argument; to urge the necessity of a case

definition: A covered vehicle used for carrying goods or people, usually roughly cuboid in shape, longer and higher than a car but smaller than a truck/lorry.
example: The van sped down the road.

definition: Alteration; change.
example: He varies his magic tricks so as to minimize the possibility that any given audience member will see the same trick twice.

definition: A vast space.
example: The Sahara desert is vast.

definition: A theater, auditorium, arena, or other area designated for sporting or entertainment events.
A main road or highway, especially in ancient Rome. (Mainly used in set phrases, below.)
An instance of having won a competition or battle or succeeded in an effort.
example: It was a great victory on the battlefield.

definition: Extreme force.
example: The violence of the storm, fortunately, was more awesome than destructive.

definition: A virtual member function of a class.
example: In fact a defeat on the battlefield, Tet was a virtual victory for the North, owing to its effect on public opinion.

definition: Any element of something that depends on sight.
Relating to, or characteristic of life.
example: vital energies; vital functions; vital actions

definition: Any of a specific group of organic compounds essential in small quantities for healthy human growth, metabolism, development, and body function; found in minute amounts in plant and animal foods or sometimes produced synthetically; deficiencies of specific vitamins produce specific disorders.
A three-dimensional measure of space that comprises a length, a width and a height. It is measured in units of cubic centimeters in metric, cubic inches or cubic feet in English measurement.
example: The room is 9x12x8, so its volume is 864 cubic feet.

definition: (often in plural) An amount of money paid to a worker for a specified quantity of work, usually calculated on an hourly basis and expressed in an amount of money per hour.
example: Before her promotion, her wages were 20% less.

definition: The condition of being weak.
example: In a small number of horses, muscle weakness may progress to paralysis.

definition: Riches; valuable material possessions.
example: She brings a wealth of knowledge to the project.

definition: A rich person.
A clause, as in legal documents, stating whereas.
example: He came first in the race, whereas his brother came last.

definition: An emphatic form of where.
example: Wherever have you been all my life?

definition: The act of speaking in a quiet voice, especially, without vibration of the vocal cords.
example: There are whispers of rebellion all around.

definition: (interrogative) What person or people; which person or people.
example: He's a person with whom I work. (defining)

definition: In a wide manner; across a wide area.
example: We walked down a wide corridor.

definition: Animals, plants, and fungi, not normally domesticated, often to the exclusion of plants, fungi, fish, insects and other invertebrates, and microscopic plants and animals; hence:
To wish, desire.
example: All the fans were willing their team to win the game.

definition: Metal formed into a thin, even thread, now usually by being drawn through a hole in a steel die.
example: This election is going to go right to the wire

definition: To become wise.
example: After Mo had a word with him, he wised up.

definition: Attestation of a fact or event; testimony.
example: She can bear witness, since she was there at the time.

definition: An injury, such as a cut, stab, or tear, to a (usually external) part of the body.
example: It took a long time to get over the wound of that insult.

definition: To enclose (an object) completely in any flexible, thin material such as fabric or paper.
example: A snake wraps itself around its prey.

definition: Each of the five regions of the earth's surface into which it was divided by climatic differences, namely the torrid zone (between the tropics), two temperate zones (between the tropics and the polar circles), and two frigid zones (within the polar circles).
example: Files in the Internet zone are blocked by default, as a security measure.
